title: Cyclosorus assamicus
intro: Cyclosorus assamicus, also known as the Assam cleft fern, is a plant species belonging to the family Blechnaceae, characterized by its distinctive leaf morphology and habitat. Traditionally, Cyclosorus assamicus has been used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant have been identified to include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cyclosorus assamicus is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and also used in the form of powder or oil, to make herbal preparations such as tea, capsules, and topical creams for medicinal purposes.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus assamicus belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae and is classified under the order Polypodiales. It is a species of fern native to India and other parts of Asia. The classification of this plant includes the following hierarchy: kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, and family Thelypteridaceae. This classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history. Further research may provide additional insights into the taxonomic relationships of Cyclosorus assamicus.
